content.text: How can we support Yash, yet another shell, with the most minimal setup? Maybe there's already a shell that has similar semantics and support. https://github.com/JanDeDobbeleer/oh-my-posh/issues/7121
I'll look at the issue and the existing shell integrations to see what fits.

input.prompt: Summarize this issue: what is being requested regarding yash shell support, any details about yash's prompt mechanisms, workarounds mentioned, and any maintainer comments.

result.output:
1	package shell
2	
3	const (
4		ZSH        = "zsh"
5		BASH       = "bash"
6		PWSH       = "pwsh"
7		FISH       = "fish"
8		CMD        = "cmd"
9		NU         = "nu"
10		GENERIC    = "shell"
11		ELVISH     = "elvish"
12		XONSH      = "xonsh"
13		CLAUDE     = "claude"
14		COPILOTCLI = "copilot-cli"
15	)
16

1	package shell
2	
3	import (
4		_ "embed"
5		"fmt"
6		"strings"
7	)
8	
9	//go:embed scripts/omp.bash
10	var bashInit string
11	
12	func (f Features) Bash() Code {
13		switch f {
14		case CursorPositioning:
15			return unixCursorPositioning
16		case FTCSMarks:
17			return unixFTCSMarks
18		case Upgrade:
19			return unixUpgrade
20		case Notice:
21			return unixNotice
22		case RPrompt:
23			if !bashBLEsession {
24				return ""
25			}
26	
27			return `bleopt prompt_rps1='$(
28		"$_omp_executable" print right \
29			--save-cache \
30			--shell=bash \
31			--shell-version="$BASH_VERSION" \
32			--status="$_omp_status" \
33			--pipestatus="${_omp_pipestatus[*]}" \
34			--no-status="$_omp_no_status" \
35			--execution-time="$_omp_execution_time" \
36			--job-count="$_omp_job_count" \
37			--stack-count="$_omp_stack_count" \
38			--terminal-width="${COLUMNS-0}" \
39			--escape=false
40	)'`
41		case Transient:
42			if !bashBLEsession {
43				return ""
44			}
45	
46			return `bleopt prompt_ps1_transient=always
47	bleopt prompt_ps1_final='$(
48	    "$_omp_executable" print transient \
49	        --shell=bash \
50	        --shell-version="$BASH_VERSION" \
51	        --escape=false
52	)'`
53		case PromptMark, PoshGit, Azure, LineError, Jobs, Tooltips, Async, Streaming, KeyHandlers, VIMode:
54			fallthrough
55		default:
56			return ""
57		}
58	}
59	
60	func QuotePosixStr(str string) string {
61		if str == "" {
62			return "''"
63		}
64	
65		return fmt.Sprintf("$'%s'", strings.NewReplacer(`\`, `\\`, "'", `\'`).Replace(str))
66	}
67

1	export POSH_SHELL='bash'
2	export POSH_SHELL_VERSION=$BASH_VERSION
3	export POWERLINE_COMMAND='oh-my-posh'
4	export CONDA_PROMPT_MODIFIER=false
5	export OSTYPE=$OSTYPE
6	
7	# disable all known python virtual environment prompts
8	export VIRTUAL_ENV_DISABLE_PROMPT=1
9	export PYENV_VIRTUALENV_DISABLE_PROMPT=1
10	
11	# global variables
12	_omp_start_time=''
13	_omp_stack_count=0
14	_omp_job_count=0
15	_omp_execution_time=-1
16	_omp_no_status=true
17	_omp_status=0
18	_omp_pipestatus=0
19	_omp_executable=::OMP::
20	
21	# switches to enable/disable features
22	_omp_cursor_positioning=0
23	_omp_ftcs_marks=0
24	
25	# start timer on command start
26	PS0='${_omp_start_time:0:$((_omp_start_time="$(_omp_milliseconds)",0))}$(_omp_ftcs_command_start)'
27	
28	# set secondary prompt
29	_omp_secondary_prompt=$(
30	    "$_omp_executable" print secondary \
31	        --shell=bash \
32	        --shell-version="$BASH_VERSION"
33	)
34	
35	function _omp_set_cursor_position() {
36	    # not supported in Midnight Commander
37	    # see https://github.com/JanDeDobbeleer/oh-my-posh/issues/3415
38	    if [[ $_omp_cursor_positioning == 0 ]] || [[ -v MC_SID ]]; then
39	        return
40	    fi
41	
42	    local oldstty=$(stty -g)
43	    stty raw -echo min 0
44	
45	    local COL
46	    local ROW
47	    IFS=';' read -rsdR -p $'\E[6n' ROW COL
48	
49	    stty "$oldstty"
50	
51	    export POSH_CURSOR_LINE=${ROW#*[}
52	    export POSH_CURSOR_COLUMN=${COL}
53	}
54	
55	function _omp_milliseconds() {
56	    if ((BASH_VERSINFO[0] >= 5)); then
57	        # EPOCHREALTIME is epoch time with microsecond precision and a
58	        # locale-dependent decimal separator, strip anything but the digits
59	        local epoch_micros=${EPOCHREALTIME//[!0-9]/}
60	        echo $((epoch_micros / 1000))
61	        return
62	    fi
63	
64	    # EPOCHREALTIME requires bash 5.0 or newer
65	    "$_omp_executable" get millis
66	}
67	
68	function _omp_ftcs_command_start() {
69	    if [[ $_omp_ftcs_marks == 1 ]]; then
70	        printf '\e]133;C\a'
71	    fi
72	}
73	
74	# template function for context loading
75	function set_poshcontext() {
76	    return
77	}
78	
79	function _omp_get_primary() {
80	    # Avoid unexpected expansions when we're generating the prompt below.
81	    shopt -u promptvars
82	    trap 'shopt -s promptvars' RETURN
83	
84	    local prompt
85	    if shopt -oq posix; then
86	        # Disable in POSIX mode.
87	        prompt='[NOTICE: Oh My Posh prompt is not supported in POSIX mode]\n\u@\h:\w\$ '
88	    else
89	        prompt=$(
90	            "$_omp_executable" print primary \
91	                --save-cache \
92	                --shell=bash \
93	                --shell-version="$BASH_VERSION" \
94	                --status="$_omp_status" \
95	                --pipestatus="${_omp_pipestatus[*]}" \
96	                --no-status="$_omp_no_status" \
97	                --execution-time="$_omp_execution_time" \
98	                --job-count="$_omp_job_count" \
99	                --stack-count="$_omp_stack_count" \
100	                --terminal-width="${COLUMNS-0}" |
101	                tr -d '\0'
102	        )
103	    fi
104	    echo "${prompt@P}"
105	}
106	
107	function _omp_get_secondary() {
108	    # Avoid unexpected expansions when we're generating the prompt below.
109	    shopt -u promptvars
110	    trap 'shopt -s promptvars' RETURN
111	
112	    if shopt -oq posix; then
113	        # Disable in POSIX mode.
114	        echo '> '
115	    else
116	        echo "${_omp_secondary_prompt@P}"
117	    fi
118	}
119	
120	function _omp_hook() {
121	    _omp_status=$? _omp_pipestatus=("${PIPESTATUS[@]}")
122	
123	    if [[ -v BP_PIPESTATUS && ${#BP_PIPESTATUS[@]} -ge ${#_omp_pipestatus[@]} ]]; then
124	        _omp_pipestatus=("${BP_PIPESTATUS[@]}")
125	    fi
126	
127	    _omp_stack_count=$((${#DIRSTACK[@]} - 1))
128	    local _omp_jobs=()
129	    _omp_jobs=($(jobs -p 2>/dev/null))
130	    _omp_job_count=${#_omp_jobs[@]}
131	
132	    _omp_execution_time=-1
133	    if [[ $_omp_start_time ]]; then
134	        local omp_now=$(_omp_milliseconds)
135	        _omp_execution_time=$((omp_now - _omp_start_time))
136	        _omp_no_status=false
137	    fi
138	    _omp_start_time=''
139	
140	    if [[ ${_omp_pipestatus[-1]} != "$_omp_status" ]]; then
141	        _omp_pipestatus=("$_omp_status")
142	    fi
143	
144	    set_poshcontext
145	    _omp_set_cursor_position
146	
147	    PS1='$(_omp_get_primary)'
148	    PS2='$(_omp_get_secondary)'
149	
150	    # Ensure that command substitution works in a prompt string.
151	    shopt -s promptvars
152	
153	    return $_omp_status
154	}
155	
156	function _omp_install_hook() {
157	    local cmd
158	    local prompt_command
159	
160	    for cmd in "${PROMPT_COMMAND[@]}"; do
161	        # skip initializing when we're already initialized
162	        if [[ $cmd = _omp_hook ]]; then
163	            return
164	        fi
165	
166	        # check if the command starts with source, if so, do not add it again
167	        # this is done to avoid issues with sourcing the same file multiple times
168	        if [[ $cmd = source* ]]; then
169	            continue
170	        fi
171	
172	        prompt_command+=("$cmd")
173	    done
174	
175	    # When VS Code shell integration is active, prepend so _omp_hook sets PS1 before
176	    # VS Code's __vsc_prompt_cmd_original wraps it with A/B shell integration sequences.
177	    if [[ "${TERM_PROGRAM-}" == "vscode" ]]; then
178	        PROMPT_COMMAND=(_omp_hook "${prompt_command[@]}")
179	    else
180	        PROMPT_COMMAND=("${prompt_command[@]}" _omp_hook)
181	    fi
182	}
183	
184	_omp_install_hook
185

input.prompt: How does yash handle the PS1/YASH_PS1 prompt variables? Does it perform parameter expansion and command substitution on them? What backslash escape sequences are supported, especially for non-printing characters / ANSI escape sequences (equivalent of bash's \[ \])? Mention \f font sequences if described.

result.output:
# YASH PS1/YASH_PS1 Prompt Handling

Based on the provided documentation, I cannot find specific information about how yash handles parameter expansion or command substitution within PS1/YASH_PS1 variables.

The document does mention that there is a `PS1S` variable for styling the "typed" portion of predicted commands and a `PS1P` variable for the "predicted" portion, but these relate to command line prediction styling rather than prompt processing details.

## Backslash Escape Sequences

The documentation extensively covers escape sequences for the **bindkey built-in** (used in line-editing key bindings), including sequences like `\H` (Home), `\E` (End), `\D` (Down arrow), and `\F00` through `\F63` for function keys. However, **the document does not describe escape sequences for prompt processing or ANSI formatting** (no equivalents to bash's `\[` and `\]` bracketing non-printing characters are mentioned).

There is no mention of `\f` font sequences in this documentation.

For comprehensive details on prompt variable handling in yash, you would need to consult additional documentation beyond this line-editing guide.

input.prompt: How does yash process the PS1 / YASH_PS1 prompt variables? Does it perform parameter expansion and command substitution on the value? List the backslash escape sequences supported in prompts, especially \f font/style sequences and how raw terminal escape sequences or non-printing characters are handled. Also describe PROMPT_COMMAND support in yash.

result.output:
# Yash PS1/YASH_PS1 Prompt Processing

## Expansion and Substitution

Yash performs three types of expansions on prompt variables: "parameter expansion, command substitution, and arithmetic expansion" (though POSIX requires only parameter expansion).

## Supported Escape Sequences

Standard sequences include:
- `\a` (bell), `\e` (escape), `\n` (newline), `\r` (carriage return)
- `\!` (history number), `\$` (# or $ based on UID)
- `\\` (backslash)

## Font/Style Sequences

The `\ffontspecs.` notation supports color and styling when line-editing is active:
- **Colors**: k (black), r (red), g (green), y (yellow), b (blue), m (magenta), c (cyan), w (white)
- **Background**: K-W (uppercase variants)
- **Styles**: t (brighter), d (normal), s (standout), u (underlined), v (reversed), b (blink), i (dim), o (bold), x (invisible), D (reset)

If line-editing is inactive or unsupported, these notations are "silently ignored."

## Non-Printing Character Handling

The `\[` and `\]` notations surround invisible content, ensuring the shell "counts the number of characters that is displayed on the terminal" correctly, preventing misalignment from special characters.

## PROMPT_COMMAND Support

Outside POSIXly-correct mode, "the value of the PROMPT_COMMAND variable is executed before each prompt." Additionally, POST_PROMPT_COMMAND executes after line input, with the COMMAND variable containing the input line for potential modification.

1	package shell
2	
3	import "fmt"
4	
5	type Features uint
6	
7	const (
8		Jobs Features = 1 << iota
9		Azure
10		PoshGit
11		LineError
12		Tooltips
13		Transient
14		FTCSMarks
15		Upgrade
16		Notice
17		PromptMark
18		RPrompt
19		CursorPositioning
20		Async
21		Streaming
22		KeyHandlers
23		VIMode
24	)
25	
26	// getAllFeatures returns all defined feature flags by iterating through bit positions
27	func getAllFeatures() []Features {
28		var features []Features
29	
30		// Iterate through all possible bit positions
31		for i := range uint(32) { // 32 should be more than enough for our features
32			feature := Features(1 << i)
33	
34			// Stop when we reach a power of 2 greater than our highest defined feature
35			if feature > VIMode*2 {
36				break
37			}
38	
39			// Add the feature if it's a power of 2 (valid feature flag)
40			if feature != 0 && (feature&(feature-1)) == 0 {
41				features = append(features, feature)
42			}
43		}
44	
45		return features
46	}
47	
48	func (f Features) Lines(shell string) Lines {
49		var lines Lines
50	
51		// Get all features dynamically
52		allFeatures := getAllFeatures()
53	
54		for _, feature := range allFeatures {
55			// Check if this feature is enabled in the Features bitmask
56			if uint(f)&uint(feature) == 0 {
57				continue
58			}
59	
60			var code Code
61	
62			switch shell {
63			case PWSH:
64				code = feature.Pwsh()
65			case ZSH:
66				code = feature.Zsh()
67			case BASH:
68				code = feature.Bash()
69			case ELVISH:
70				code = feature.Elvish()
71			case FISH:
72				code = feature.Fish()
73			case CMD:
74				code = feature.Cmd()
75			case NU:
76				code = feature.Nu()
77			case XONSH:
78				code = feature.Xonsh()
79			}
80	
81			if len(code) > 0 {
82				lines = append(lines, code)
83			}
84		}
85	
86		return lines
87	}
88	
89	func (f Features) String() string {
90		return fmt.Sprintf("%b", uint(f))
91	}
92

1	package shell
2	
3	type Formats struct {
4		Escape     string
5		Left       string
6		Linechange string
7		ClearBelow string
8		ClearLine  string
9	
10		Title string
11	
12		SaveCursorPosition    string
13		RestoreCursorPosition string
14	
15		Osc99 string
16		Osc7  string
17		Osc51 string
18	
19		EscapeSequences map[rune]string
20	
21		HyperlinkStart  string
22		HyperlinkCenter string
23		HyperlinkEnd    string
24	
25		ITermPromptMark string
26		ITermCurrentDir string
27		ITermRemoteHost string
28	}
29	
30	func GetFormats(shell string) *Formats {
31		var formats *Formats
32	
33		switch shell {
34		case BASH:
35			formats = &Formats{
36				Escape:                "\\[%s\\]",
37				Linechange:            "\\[\x1b[%d%s\\]",
38				Left:                  "\\[\x1b[%dD\\]",
39				ClearBelow:            "\\[\x1b[0J\\]",
40				ClearLine:             "\\[\x1b[K\\]",
41				SaveCursorPosition:    "\\[\x1b7\\]",
42				RestoreCursorPosition: "\\[\x1b8\\]",
43				Title:                 "\\[\x1b]0;%s\007\\]",
44				HyperlinkStart:        "\\[\x1b]8;;",
45				HyperlinkCenter:       "\x1b\\\\\\]",
46				HyperlinkEnd:          "\\[\x1b]8;;\x1b\\\\\\]",
47				Osc99:                 "\\[\x1b]9;9;%s\x1b\\\\\\]",
48				Osc7:                  "\\[\x1b]7;file://%s/%s\x1b\\\\\\]",
49				Osc51:                 "\\[\x1b]51;A;%s@%s:%s\x1b\\\\\\]",
50				ITermPromptMark:       "\\[$(iterm2_prompt_mark)\\]",
51				ITermCurrentDir:       "\\[\x1b]1337;CurrentDir=%s\x07\\]",
52				ITermRemoteHost:       "\\[\x1b]1337;RemoteHost=%s@%s\x07\\]",
53				EscapeSequences: map[rune]string{
54					'\\': `\\`,
55				},
56			}
57		case ZSH:
58			formats = &Formats{
59				Escape:                "%%{%s%%}",
60				Linechange:            "%%{\x1b[%d%s%%}",
61				Left:                  "%%{\x1b[%dD%%}",
62				ClearBelow:            "%{\x1b[0J%}",
63				ClearLine:             "%{\x1b[K%}",
64				SaveCursorPosition:    "%{\x1b7%}",
65				RestoreCursorPosition: "%{\x1b8%}",
66				Title:                 "%%{\x1b]0;%s\007%%}",
67				HyperlinkStart:        "%{\x1b]8;;",
68				HyperlinkCenter:       "\x1b\\%}",
69				HyperlinkEnd:          "%{\x1b]8;;\x1b\\%}",
70				Osc99:                 "%%{\x1b]9;9;%s\x1b\\%%}",
71				Osc7:                  "%%{\x1b]7;file://%s/%s\x1b\\%%}",
72				Osc51:                 "%%{\x1b]51;A%s@%s:%s\x1b\\%%}",
73				ITermPromptMark:       "%{$(iterm2_prompt_mark)%}",
74				ITermCurrentDir:       "%%{\x1b]1337;CurrentDir=%s\x07%%}",
75				ITermRemoteHost:       "%%{\x1b]1337;RemoteHost=%s@%s\x07%%}",
76				EscapeSequences: map[rune]string{
77					'%': "%%",
78				},
79			}
80		default:
81			formats = &Formats{
82				Escape:                "%s",
83				Linechange:            "\x1b[%d%s",
84				Left:                  "\x1b[%dD",
85				ClearBelow:            "\x1b[0J",
86				ClearLine:             "\x1b[K",
87				SaveCursorPosition:    "\x1b7",
88				RestoreCursorPosition: "\x1b8",
89				Title:                 "\x1b]0;%s\007",
90				// when in fish on Linux, it seems hyperlinks ending with \\ print a \
91				// unlike on macOS. However, this is a fish bug, so do not try to fix it here:
92				// https://github.com/JanDeDobbeleer/oh-my-posh/pull/3288#issuecomment-1369137068
93				HyperlinkStart:  "\x1b]8;;",
94				HyperlinkCenter: "\x1b\\",
95				HyperlinkEnd:    "\x1b]8;;\x1b\\",
96				Osc99:           "\x1b]9;9;%s\x1b\\",
97				Osc7:            "\x1b]7;file://%s/%s\x1b\\",
98				Osc51:           "\x1b]51;A%s@%s:%s\x1b\\",
99				ITermCurrentDir: "\x1b]1337;CurrentDir=%s\x07",
100				ITermRemoteHost: "\x1b]1337;RemoteHost=%s@%s\x07",
101			}
102		}
103	
104		return formats
105	}
106

content.text:
Yash maps almost one-to-one onto the bash integration — it's the closest existing shell by far, and in two ways it's actually easier than bash. Here's my assessment for [#7121](https://github.com/JanDeDobbeleer/oh-my-posh/issues/7121).

## Why bash is the right template

From the yash manual (interact/lineedit docs):

- **`PROMPT_COMMAND`** — yash executes it before each prompt, exactly like bash (yash ≥ 2.39, outside POSIXly-correct mode). Same hook model as [omp.bash](src/shell/scripts/omp.bash).
- **`\[` / `\]`** — yash uses the same non-printing-sequence markers as bash, so the existing bash `Formats` in [formats.go:34](src/shell/formats.go:34) work unchanged.
- **Prompt expansion** — yash subjects `PS1` to parameter expansion, command substitution, *and* arithmetic expansion on every prompt draw. So `PS1='$(_omp_get_primary)'` just works, no `${var@P}` re-expansion trick needed.

And two bonuses bash doesn't have:

- **`YASH_PS1R`** — a native right prompt. Bash needs ble.sh for RPrompt; yash gives it for free.
- **`POST_PROMPT_COMMAND`** — runs after a line is accepted, before execution. That's a real preexec hook, so execution time works without bash's `PS0` hack.

`YASH_PS1`/`YASH_PS1R`/`YASH_PS2` take precedence over `PS1` in yash, which is the polite variant to set — a shared `.profile` sourced by other POSIX shells stays unaffected.

## What can't come over from omp.bash

The script needs a POSIX-ified trim, since yash won't accept the bashisms: arrays (`PROMPT_COMMAND` is a scalar string in yash), `PIPESTATUS`/`BP_PIPESTATUS` (no equivalent — pass only `--status`), `PS0`, `${var@P}`, `shopt`, `${EPOCHREALTIME}`. Timing falls back to `oh-my-posh get millis` (already the pre-bash-5 path), captured in `POST_PROMPT_COMMAND`. Job count via `jobs | wc -l`.

## Minimal touch points

1. `src/shell/scripts/omp.yash` — new ~60-line POSIX script (sketch below)
2. [constants.go](src/shell/constants.go) — add `YASH = "yash"`
3. `src/shell/yash.go` — embed the script + `Features.Yash()` (RPrompt via `YASH_PS1R`, reuse `unixUpgrade`/`unixNotice`/`unixFTCSMarks`; Transient/Tooltips/KeyHandlers return empty — yash's `bindkey` only binds built-in editing commands, no user widgets)
4. [init.go](src/shell/init.go) — add `YASH` to the `Init` and `generateScript` switches (`QuotePosixStr`), source with `.` (yash has no `source` builtin), and add it to the `ZSH, BASH` case in `sessionScript`
5. [features.go:62](src/shell/features.go:62) — add the dispatch case
6. [formats.go:34](src/shell/formats.go:34) — `case BASH, YASH:`
7. [writer.go:268](src/terminal/writer.go:268) — add `YASH` to the bash/zsh title-escaping case
8. [cli/init.go:25](src/cli/init.go:25) `supportedShells` + website docs

Script sketch:

```sh
export POSH_SHELL='yash'
_omp_executable=::OMP::
_omp_status=0 _omp_no_status=true _omp_execution_time=-1 _omp_start_time=''

_omp_hook() {
    _omp_status=$?
    _omp_execution_time=-1
    if [ -n "$_omp_start_time" ]; then
        _omp_execution_time=$(($("$_omp_executable" get millis) - _omp_start_time))
        _omp_no_status=false
    fi
    _omp_start_time=''
    return $_omp_status
}

PROMPT_COMMAND='_omp_hook'
POST_PROMPT_COMMAND='_omp_start_time=$("$_omp_executable" get millis)'

YASH_PS1='$("$_omp_executable" print primary --save-cache --shell=yash \
    --status=$_omp_status --no-status=$_omp_no_status \
    --execution-time=$_omp_execution_time \
    --job-count=$(jobs | grep -c .) --terminal-width=${COLUMNS:-0})'
YASH_PS2='$("$_omp_executable" print secondary --shell=yash)'
```

## Caveats to verify on a real yash

- **Minimum version**: `PROMPT_COMMAND` landed in 2.39 (2015); `POST_PROMPT_COMMAND` is newer (~2.52). Worth a version check or graceful degradation of execution time.
- **Backslash order**: yash expands the prompt first, then interprets `\`-escapes on the result. That's what makes `\[ \]` from the omp output work, but it also means a literal `\` in rendered segment text gets eaten — same class of problem bash had before the `@P` approach. Probably acceptable at launch; the `EscapeSequences` map handles the title already.
- **POSIX mode**: like omp.bash's guard, the script should no-op when yash runs POSIXly-correct (invoked as `sh`), since `PROMPT_COMMAND` and `YASH_PS1` are ignored there anyway.

The issue's suggestion to "rework bash for broader compatibility" I'd skip: omp.bash leans on `PS0`, `PIPESTATUS`, and `@P` for good reasons, and degrading it to POSIX would cost bash users features. A small dedicated `omp.yash` that shares bash's escaping formats is cheaper and safer than unifying the two scripts. If a `.sh`-style generic POSIX integration is ever wanted, this yash script minus the yash-specific variables would be the starting point.

Want me to implement it? I can build the whole thing, though I can't run yash on this Windows machine — verification would need yash under WSL (same harness as the fish/zsh streaming tests).
